timeline_funding.title: EEC 52 - Canada Charity Partners
timeline_funding.description: Through the application of national and local priorities, the CSJ program seeks to provide youth, particularly those who face barriers to employment with access to work opportunities. Funded employers must demonstrate that they are providing quality work experiences for youth that provide opportunities to develop and improve their skills.

timeline_funding.description: Through the application of national and local priorities, the CSJ program seeks to provide youth who face barriers to employment with access to work opportunities. Funded employers must demonstrate that they are providing quality work experiences for youth that provide opportunities to develop and improve their skills. The CSJ program is delivered by the Department of ESD.

shared_directors.legal_name: Centre Famille SNAP / SNAP Family Center

briefing_memo: Canada Charity Partners (QC) has received $55,772 in federal funding to date. The CRA revoked the organization's registered charity status following audit findings that it failed to implement corrective measures from a 2020 compliance agreement and continued non-compliance with the Income Tax Act during the 2020-2021 audit period (canadiancharitylaw.ca, justpeaceadvocates.ca, thecjn.ca, all February 2026). T3010 filings show one violation on record and an overhead ratio reaching 14.7%, though the forensic risk score of 32 remains moderate. Elevated due-diligence review is strongly recommended before any further disbursement, given the revoked charitable status represents a material regulatory barrier to lawful operation as a charity.

actions.title: Verify charitable registration status with CRA
actions.rationale: Multiple HIGH-severity news sources report CRA revocation of charitable status for this organization.

actions.title: Suspend all pending and future disbursements
actions.rationale: Organization appears to have lost charitable registration, making it ineligible for federal funding as a qualified donee.

actions.title: Initiate recovery proceedings for federal funding already disbursed
actions.rationale: Federal funds totaling $55,772 may have been provided to an entity that no longer holds valid charitable status.

actions.title: Request detailed CRA audit findings and revocation documentation
actions.rationale: CRA audit and revocation provide official basis for funding ineligibility and may reveal misuse of prior funds.
